description: Roles and responsibilities spreadsheet with an approver/creator conflict
document_excerpt:
Role, Responsibilities
IT Security Manager, Creates and updates security controls; runs quarterly control assessments; approves control exceptions
Compliance Analyst, Uploads audit evidence; prepares assessment workpapers
HR Administrator, Creates user accounts; assigns system roles; deactivates leavers

output.summary: The document defines three roles. The IT Security Manager both implements controls and assesses them, a self-review conflict. The HR Administrator combines account creation with role assignment, which permits single-handed provisioning of privileged accounts. The Compliance Analyst's duties are compatible and map cleanly to evidence and assessment permissions.

roles.name: IT Security Manager
roles.description: Owns security control lifecycle and exception approval
roles.notes: Holding both controls.write and assessments.write enables self-review

roles.name: HR Administrator
roles.notes: Combines account creation with role assignment

sod_conflicts.title: Control implementer assesses own controls
sod_conflicts.description: The IT Security Manager both creates/updates controls and runs the assessments that judge them, so control weaknesses can be self-certified.
sod_conflicts.recommendation: Move assessment execution to the Compliance Analyst or an independent assessor role.

sod_conflicts.title: Single-handed privileged provisioning
sod_conflicts.description: The HR Administrator can create an account and grant it any role without a second person involved.
sod_conflicts.recommendation: Split role assignment to a separate approver, or require a second-person review for privileged role grants.

suggested_platform_roles.name: Compliance Analyst
suggested_platform_roles.description: Evidence collection and assessment workpaper preparation

suggested_sod_rules.name: Account creation combined with role assignment
suggested_sod_rules.description: Mirrors the HR Administrator conflict found in the document: one account holding both duties can self-provision privileged users.

gaps_and_risks.title: No documented reviewer for exception approvals
gaps_and_risks.detail: Control exceptions are approved by the same role that owns the controls; the document names no independent reviewer or escalation path.

description: SoD matrix fragment marking incompatible duty pairs
document_excerpt:
Duty pairs marked X are incompatible:
, Create vendor, Approve vendor payment, Modify audit log
Create vendor, -, X, X
Approve vendor payment, X, -, X
Current assignment: Procurement Lead holds Create vendor AND Approve vendor payment.

output.summary: The matrix declares vendor creation and vendor payment approval incompatible, yet the stated current assignment gives the Procurement Lead both duties, so the organization is violating its own documented matrix. Audit log modification is correctly isolated from both duties.

roles.name: Procurement Lead
roles.notes: The platform catalog has no payment-approval permission; the conflict is at the documented-duty level

sod_conflicts.title: Documented matrix violated by current assignment
sod_conflicts.description: The organization's own matrix marks Create vendor and Approve vendor payment as incompatible, but the Procurement Lead currently holds both.
sod_conflicts.recommendation: Reassign payment approval to a finance role immediately; the violation contradicts the organization's approved SoD matrix.

gaps_and_risks.title: Matrix has no enforcement mechanism
gaps_and_risks.detail: The matrix documents incompatible pairs but nothing prevents an administrator from assigning both duties, as the current Procurement Lead assignment demonstrates.
